description:
Review governance regime: EU Code of Practice on Disinformation

Diff summary:
- evaluates the EU Code of Practice on Disinformation as a governance artifact rather than a software PR
- considers authority allocation across platforms, advertisers, fact-checkers, researchers, civil society, and regulators
- considers coercion risk under DSA-linked co-regulation, election-period rapid response, political-ad transparency, demonetisation, service integrity, user empowerment, researcher access, and monitoring obligations
- asks whether the regime appropriately constrains manipulative conduct without drifting into viewpoint suppression or over-removal

context.author: openclaw-session
changed_files: 2022 Strengthened Code of Practice on Disinformation

evidence_basis: European Commission description of 2018 and 2022 Code
Digital Strategy page describing commitments and DSA integration
2025 endorsement of Code of Conduct integration under DSA
notes.known_strength: targets coordination, manipulation infrastructure, ad transparency, and reporting
notes.known_risk: soft-compulsion toward over-removal or bureaucratic truth governance if independence and reversibility are weak
